title: Asthma follow-up

items.final_wording: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

items.rationale: Supports mapped asthma follow-up identity within DHA telehealth scope.

items.final_wording: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

items.rationale: Supports mapped asthma symptom, pattern, trigger, occupational, respiratory-examination, peak-flow, and history fields.

items.final_wording: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

items.rationale: Supports mapped severe-breathlessness, chest-symptom, syncope, inability-to-speak, cyanosis, altered-consciousness, and safety fields as prompts only.

items.final_wording: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

items.rationale: Supports mapped peak-flow and spirometry fields.

items.final_wording: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

items.rationale: Supports mapped inhaler use and technique, control, response, and clinician referral fields.

items.final_wording: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

items.rationale: Corroborates mapped symptom and objective-test fields; no duplicate item is mapped.

items.final_wording: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

items.rationale: Supports mapped exacerbation and control-review fields.

items.final_wording: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1 and 1.14.1–1.14.2 — inhaler technique, adherence, and action plans

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

items.rationale: Supports mapped trigger and inhaler-technique discussion without medicine names or doses.
